sections.paragraph:
γνησίως adv. of γνήσιος (Eur. et al.; ins, pap, LXX) sincerely, genuinely (so the horoscope in PLond I, 130, 3 p. 133 [I/II a.d.]; PTebt 326, 11; 2 Macc 14:8; 3 Macc 3:23) μεριμνᾶν Phil 2:20 (cp. IMagnMai 188, 8ff).—M-M s.v. γνήσιος.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IMagnMai IMagnMai = Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander—Lists 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 202
